threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think London’s all about the tube. Try getting anywhere in Hackney after 11 PM without a bus or a taxi, mate.
Last Friday on the N35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old Michael Jackson tunes. It felt like a mini concert, the driver just smiled and let it roll.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor carrying an armful of vinyl records to a pop-up café, chatting about a hidden gem market around the corner.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves vegan doner with a side of homemade vegan tzatziki. It's a late-night lifesaver!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to pack snacks for BART delays—never trust the schedules, especially when there’s a loud bang at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched two tourists argue over a dog blocking the Muni tracks while a street musician played 'Don't Stop Believin'' on a ukulele. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but they miss the hidden vinyl shops and the locals-only bars down hidden alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they stand in the middle of the sidewalk, oblivious to the flow of locals weaving around them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘London Fields’ is just a park and not the name of a small empire of overpriced coffee shops.
Caught the N137 last night. A guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. You could feel the eye rolls and smiles at the same time.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a kid argue with their mom about whether the ice cream truck's jingle counted as pop music.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a street art gallery under the U-Bahn tracks, complete with impromptu dance-offs and pop-up cafes. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows not to panic when the BART's down; they check the bus app instead, even when it says 'delayed' yet again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo get off the Muni, check his watch, and sprint into the courthouse. Only in SF do you dress fancy and still ride the bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the placa, but most locals know the best spots for brunch are tucked away in those side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when someone’s new; they’re the ones mistaking bocadillos for sandwich rolls at the local bar.
